claim: Individual developers see large productivity gains with AI tools, but adoption at the organizational level is still limited and uneven
actionable: When adopting AI tools for teams, start with individuals who are enthusiastic; expect organizational adoption to lag behind individual use by 6+ months
source_title: Software engineering with LLMs in 2025: reality check

claim: Experienced engineers (20+ years) are adopting AI coding tools more enthusiastically than earlier-career engineers in the same orgs
actionable: Senior engineers can accelerate skill acquisition and side projects by learning AI-first workflows; younger engineers may benefit from structured guidance
source_title: Software engineering with LLMs in 2025: reality check

claim: Large tech companies are preparing for 10x code throughput by investing in deployment pipelines, feature flagging, and code review tooling
actionable: Build infrastructure for high code velocity: invest in automated deployment, comprehensive feature flags, and robust code review before deploying AI-assisted teams
source_title: Software engineering with LLMs in 2025: reality check

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) adoption is accelerating—OpenAI, Google, Microsoft all added support by March–April 2025, thousands of servers now exist
actionable: Evaluate MCP servers for your integration needs; build MCP-compatible tools to connect databases, APIs, and services to agents
source_title: Software engineering with LLMs in 2025: reality check

claim: Founders and CEOs are far more enthusiastic about AI tools than senior engineers; this gap widens at AI tooling companies themselves
actionable: Understand enthusiasm gap: align engineering teams with founder vision through education and proof-of-value pilots rather than mandate adoption
source_title: Software engineering with LLMs in 2025: reality check

claim: Non-thinking, standard models hallucinate outdated information and fail on novel problems; teams find value but describe results as 'hit or miss'
actionable: For critical code, use reasoning models or add verification steps; do not rely on standard models for novel patterns or cutting-edge APIs
source_title: Software engineering with LLMs in 2025: reality check

claim: LLMs are as transformative as the shift from assembly language to high-level languages, but non-determinism is a fundamental new constraint
actionable: Treat LLM outputs as probabilistic; build verification, testing, and rollback into every LLM-assisted workflow
source_title: Software engineering with LLMs in 2025: reality check
